dimension: 容器化 agent 沙箱方案（Mac 上最好配最好用 + agent 隔离 + Computer Use + 浏览器）

options.name: cua (trycua/cua) — Docker provider + trycua/cua-ubuntu image
options.description: Open-source infrastructure purpose-built for Computer-Use Agents (MIT, 19k stars, pushed 2026-06-26 — actively maintained). On a Mac you run the trycua/cua-ubuntu container under Docker Desktop: KasmVNC + Ubuntu 22.04 XFCE with Firefox/Chromium, and the cua 'computer-server' (screenshot/click/type/scroll) auto-boots so an agent can connect immediately. Browser-based live desktop at http://localhost:8006 (no VNC client). Drive it from Claude Agent SDK / Claude Code via cua's MCP server (cua = the hands, Claude = the brain), or via the Python/TS SDK Computer() handle. Same one-line API can later escalate the SAME workload to a Lume Apple-Virtualization micro-VM if you ever need stronger isolation or a macOS guest — without changing agent code.
options.setupEffort: ~3 steps: install Docker Desktop; pip install cua-computer (+ cua-mcp-server); Computer(os_type='linux', provider_type='docker', image='trycua/cua-ubuntu:latest', name='founder-01') then await computer.run() (first run auto-pulls native arm64 image + boots desktop). Open localhost:8006 to watch/takeover.
options.isolation: Standard Docker container isolation (shared kernel of Docker Desktop's LinuxKit VM). On Mac this is already container-inside-a-hypervisor, so you inherit a VM boundary from the host for free. Clean upgrade path: switch provider_type to a Lume Apple-Virtualization micro-VM (per-container VM) for hard isolation with no code change.
options.macFit: Excellent. Native arm64 image confirmed via docker manifest inspect (multi-arch amd64+arm64) → runs natively on Apple Silicon, NO Rosetta emulation. cua's own pitch: 'no VM spin-up, behaves the same on macOS/Windows/Linux' under Docker Desktop.
pros: Actively maintained, MIT, built specifically for computer-use agents (the project's exact domain)
One container = full Linux desktop (any GUI app, not just a browser) + browser + auto-started computer-server
Native arm64 on Apple Silicon (verified) → fast, no emulation
Native MCP server → drops straight into Claude Code / Claude Agent SDK as a tool; account injection via per-container env/volumes is an extensible interface (spawn one named container per identity)
Browser-based KasmVNC live view + takeover at :8006; agent trajectories (screenshots+actions) can be recorded to a mounted dir for replay (VM7)
Container-first today, but identical API can escalate to a Lume micro-VM only if needed (matches 'prefer container, VM no short-term advantage')
cons: Slightly more than a single command (needs the Python/TS SDK or MCP wiring) vs a bare docker run
VM7 full record/replay/persist is assembled (KasmVNC live view + cua trajectory dir + ffmpeg capture + Claude Agent SDK logs), not one turnkey switch
VM8 graceful shutdown is standard Docker SIGTERM + computer.stop(); flushing state on stop is DIY
Static-egress proxy is generic Docker plumbing (HTTP(S)_PROXY env or proxy sidecar), not a built-in feature

options.name: Anthropic computer-use-demo (anthropic-quickstarts Docker image)
options.description: The reference Docker image for Claude Computer Use: Ubuntu 22.04 + Xvfb + x11vnc + noVNC + Firefox-ESR + xdotool, plus a Streamlit chat loop. Single docker run brings up a full Linux desktop with a COMBINED chat+desktop web UI at http://localhost:8080 (also Streamlit :8501, noVNC :6080, raw VNC :5900). The canonical source of the Computer Use tool loop and the fastest way to confirm screenshot/click/type works on this Mac.
options.setupEffort: Literally one command: docker run -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... -v $HOME/.anthropic:/home/computeruse/.anthropic -p 5900 -p 8501 -p 6080 -p 8080 -it ghcr.io/anthropics/anthropic-quickstarts:computer-use-demo-latest, then open localhost:8080.
options.isolation: Single Docker container, shared kernel (LinuxKit VM on Mac). Anthropic explicitly warns to run it in a dedicated VM/container with minimal privileges, allowlist egress, and never hand it real credentials (prompt-injection risk).
options.macFit: Very good for validation. Native arm64 image confirmed via docker manifest inspect (multi-arch) → runs natively on Apple Silicon. Combined UI at :8080 is the slickest out-of-box live view of the set.
pros: Easiest possible path: one docker run to a working Computer-Use desktop with live UI
Native arm64 (verified) → no emulation on Apple Silicon
Combined chat+desktop view at :8080 + noVNC at :6080 = great built-in observability for a smoke test
MIT (anthropic-quickstarts); it IS the Computer Use protocol reference, so behavior matches the API exactly
~/.anthropic volume persists API key + custom system prompt
cons: A demo, not a framework: thin agent loop, no task DB, no step-by-step replay store, no multi-account/identity interface
No built-in record/replay/persist beyond live view (VM7 must be added)
No native proxy/static-egress, no account-injection abstraction
Streamlit single-session UX; not meant as a fleet runtime

options.name: browser-use/web-ui (self-hosted Docker)
options.description: Dockerized web UI around browser-use (MIT, 100k stars, pushed 2026-06-26 — the most popular open web-agent library). docker-compose brings up a Chrome with CDP, a Gradio web UI (:7788), and a browser-based noVNC live view (:6080, VNC :5901, CDP :9222). Browser-ONLY (no general desktop), but the turnkey dockerized way to give a Claude/LLM agent an observable, controllable browser on Mac.
options.setupEffort: git clone + docker compose up -d (or pull ghcr.io/browser-use/web-ui); set provider API keys via .env; open localhost:7788 (UI) and localhost:6080 (live browser).
options.isolation: Single container, shared kernel; browser sandboxed inside the container. Same Mac container-in-LinuxKit-VM boundary as the others.
options.macFit: Good. Runs under Docker Desktop; allocates 2GB shm for Chrome stability. Confirm arm64 image / build locally on Apple Silicon if needed.
pros: Turnkey docker-compose: web UI + noVNC live view + CDP all wired
Built on the most-used open web-agent lib; broad model support incl. Anthropic via env keys
noVNC live view (observability) + CDP for programmatic control + external BROWSER_CDP support
MIT, active
cons: Browser-only — cannot run non-browser desktop apps (no general Computer Use)
Proxy not pre-wired in the compose (add via env/Chrome flags)
Recording/replay of agent steps is DIY beyond live view
Overlaps the already-decided web layer (Browser Use/Steel) rather than the desktop sandbox need

options.name: Steel (steel-dev/steel-browser) self-host
options.description: Open-source 'browser API for AI agents' (Apache-2.0, 7.2k stars, pushed 2026-06-24 — active). Self-host with docker run / docker compose: REST API :3000, UI :5173, CDP :9223; connect any Playwright/Puppeteer/Selenium client. Session management (cookies/localStorage), proxy + extension support, anti-detect, session viewer. Browser-only; already the leading candidate for the project's web layer in the infra doc.
options.setupEffort: docker run --rm -it -p 3000:3000 -p 9223:9223 ghcr.io/steel-dev/steel-browser:latest, or docker compose up -d for API(:3000)+UI(:5173). Connect via CDP ws.
options.isolation: Single container, shared kernel; Mac container-in-LinuxKit-VM boundary. Browser sandbox only.
options.macFit: OK with a caveat: docs note ARM has known issues — use official ARM images or build locally on Apple Silicon. Verify arm64 before committing.
pros: Purpose-built browser sandbox with first-class CDP + proxy + session persistence + session viewer
Apache-2.0, active, batteries-included; pairs cleanly as the web layer under a desktop sandbox
Native proxy/extension support eases static-egress + anti-block plumbing
cons: Browser-only — no general desktop Computer Use
ARM/Apple Silicon flagged as needing official ARM image or local build
Recording/replay beyond session viewer is DIY
Solves the web slice, not the 'full computer use desktop' requirement

options.name: Bytebot (bytebot-ai/bytebot)
options.description: Self-hosted AI desktop agent: one docker compose brings up Ubuntu 22.04 + XFCE + Firefox + VS Code, an agent service (NestJS), a Tasks UI (:9992), agent API (:9991), desktop/computer-use + MCP + VNC (:9990), with Live Desktop View and Takeover Mode. Was the prior turnkey favorite — but the repo is ARCHIVED (GitHub archived:true, last push 2025-09-12). Apache-2.0.
options.setupEffort: git clone; echo ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... > docker/.env; docker-compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up -d; open localhost:9992. (Proxy variant: docker-compose.proxy.yml with LiteLLM.)
options.isolation: Each desktop in its own Docker container, separated from host; shared kernel (LinuxKit VM on Mac). Anthropic-style prompt-injection cautions apply.
options.macFit: Worked under Docker Desktop (no Mac-specific docs); but archived status means no Apple-Silicon/security upkeep going forward.
pros: Most turnkey out-of-box UX of the set: Tasks UI + Live View + Takeover + multi-model (LiteLLM/Ollama) in one compose
Apache-2.0, includes desktop apps + MCP + REST + VNC
Closest thing to a finished 'founder desktop' product
cons: ARCHIVED (no maintenance/security updates) — disqualifying as a primary base for a long-lived project
Adopt only by hard-forking and owning the code yourself
No native static-egress/anti-block; observability is live view + container logs (no built-in replay store)

options.name: cua via Lume / Lumier (Apple Virtualization VM) — fallback for macOS guest only
options.description: Same cua stack but backed by a full Linux/macOS VM via Apple's Virtualization.framework (Lume CLI, or Lumier as a Docker-style wrapper). This is the ONLY way to run a real macOS guest (for Mac-only apps) or to get a per-agent hardware-isolated VM. Apple licensing caps ~2 macOS guests per physical Mac.
options.setupEffort: curl install Lume; lume run macos-sequoia-vanilla:latest (or a linux image); connect via cua Computer(provider_type='lume'/'cloud'). Heavier than Docker (full VM image download + boot).
options.isolation: Strongest: each guest is a true Apple-Virtualization VM (separate kernel), not a shared-kernel container.
options.macFit: Apple-Silicon-native (Virtualization.framework) but heavyweight; only justified when you need macOS apps or per-agent VM isolation.
pros: Hard VM isolation per agent; only path to a macOS guest
Same cua SDK/MCP API as the Docker provider — switch with one parameter
Apple-Silicon native virtualization
cons: Heavyweight VM — the user explicitly said a VM has no short-term advantage; skip unless macOS-app or hard isolation is required
~2 macOS guests per physical Mac (Apple licensing); larger footprint and slower boot than a container
Overkill for the MVP on a single dev Mac

recommendation.pick: cua (trycua/cua) running the trycua/cua-ubuntu container under Docker Desktop, driven by Claude Agent SDK / Claude Code through cua's MCP server. Use the Anthropic computer-use-demo image as the 1-command Day-0 smoke test.
recommendation.why: It is the only candidate that is (a) actively maintained and purpose-built for computer-use agents (MIT, pushed within a day), (b) a single container giving a FULL Linux desktop + browser + auto-started screenshot/click/type server with a browser-based live view at :8006, (c) native arm64 on Apple Silicon (verified via docker manifest inspect, no Rosetta), and (d) natively wired into Claude Code / Claude Agent SDK via its MCP server so Claude stays the brain and cua is the hands. It is container-first today (matching 'prefer container, a heavyweight VM has no short-term advantage') yet the SAME one-line API can escalate a workload to a Lume Apple-Virtualization micro-VM later if hard isolation or a macOS guest is ever needed. The prior turnkey favorite, Bytebot, is now archived (verified archived:true, last push 2025-09-12) and should not be a base for a long-lived project. Account injection is an extensible interface: spawn one named container per identity with per-identity env + mounted volume (secrets from the already-decided self-hosted Infisical); static egress (already-decided Decodo/IPRoyal ISP IP) attaches transparently via HTTP(S)_PROXY env or a proxy sidecar so all desktop+browser traffic inherits the static IP.
recommendation.setupSketch: 1) Docker Desktop: brew install --cask docker; launch; raise CPU/RAM in Resources. 2) pip install cua-computer cua-mcp-server (or cua-agent[all]). 3) Boot one founder sandbox: Computer(os_type='linux', provider_type='docker', image='trycua/cua-ubuntu:latest', name='founder-01'); await computer.run() — auto-pulls the native arm64 image and starts XFCE + Firefox + computer-server. 4) Observe/takeover at http://localhost:8006 (KasmVNC in browser). 5) Register the cua MCP server in Claude Code / Claude Agent SDK so the orchestrator calls screenshot/click/type as tools (Claude = brain, cua = hands). 6) Account/secrets injection (extensible): per identity, launch a new named container with env={...} + a mounted ./identities/<id> volume; pull values at boot from self-hosted Infisical — add accounts on demand, none preconfigured. 7) Static egress proxy: set HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY (Decodo/IPRoyal ISP IP) in the container env, or route the container through a proxy sidecar/gateway, so all egress inherits the fixed IP transparently. 8) VM7 (observe+record+replay+persist): (i) KasmVNC live view at :8006; (ii) record agent trajectories (screenshots+actions+tool I/O) to a mounted ./trajectories volume; (iii) Claude Agent SDK already persists full message/tool transcripts; (iv) optionally ffmpeg-record the KasmVNC stream for pixel-exact replay — all written to host volumes for persistence. 9) VM8 (graceful shutdown): trap SIGTERM in the orchestrator -> flush logs/trajectory + await computer.stop() -> docker stop (Docker sends SIGTERM then waits the grace period); named container + mounted volume = resume on restart. 10) Fast validator before all this: one docker run of ghcr.io/anthropics/anthropic-quickstarts:computer-use-demo-latest (-p 8080:8080) to confirm Computer Use works on this Mac in a single command.

keyInsights: Bytebot — the prior turnkey favorite in the infra doc — is now ARCHIVED (GitHub archived:true, last push 2025-09-12, despite 11k stars). It still works but gets no security/Apple-Silicon upkeep; only adopt by hard-forking. This is the single biggest correction to the earlier research.

Docker Desktop on Mac runs every Linux container inside a LinuxKit hypervisor VM, so a 'container' on a Mac is already container-inside-a-VM — you inherit a VM-grade boundary from the host for free. This directly validates the user's constraint that a separate heavyweight per-agent VM 'has no short-term advantage'; the container path already gives strong host isolation.

Verified via docker manifest inspect: both trycua/cua-ubuntu:latest and ghcr.io/anthropics/anthropic-quickstarts:computer-use-demo-latest ship native arm64 manifests, so they run natively on Apple Silicon with NO Rosetta/QEMU emulation. cua's docs showing --platform=linux/amd64 is just an example, not a limitation.

Two distinct 'computer use' layers: (a) full desktop = the Xvfb+xdotool / cua-computer-server + noVNC/KasmVNC pattern (cua, Anthropic demo, Bytebot) which can drive ANY GUI app; (b) browser-only = CDP-based (Steel, browser-use/web-ui). The project needs (a); the already-decided web layer (Browser Use/Steel) covers (b) and pairs underneath.

No single container is turnkey for the new VM7/VM8 requirements. VM7 (observe+record+replay+persist) must be assembled: live view (KasmVNC/noVNC) + agent trajectory dir + Claude Agent SDK transcripts + optional ffmpeg screen capture, all on host volumes. VM8 (graceful shutdown) = SIGTERM trap -> flush state + computer.stop() -> docker stop. cua gives the cleanest hooks for both.

Recommended architecture is separation of concerns: Claude Agent SDK / Claude Code = orchestrator/brain; cua MCP server + cua-ubuntu container = the sandbox 'hands'. This keeps the project on its decided Claude foundation while making the sandbox swappable (Docker today, Lume micro-VM later) without changing agent code.

Account/secrets injection should be per-container (one named container per identity, env + mounted volume sourced from the already-decided self-hosted Infisical) — an extensible add-on-demand interface, not a preconfigured monolith. Static egress (Decodo/IPRoyal ISP IP) attaches transparently via HTTP(S)_PROXY env or a proxy sidecar, inherited by both desktop and browser traffic.

A macOS GUEST (for Mac-only apps) is the ONE thing Docker cannot provide — it requires Lume/Apple Virtualization (a true VM, ~2 macOS guests per physical Mac by Apple licensing). Default to the Linux desktop container; only reach for the VM if a Mac-only app is genuinely required.

dimension: 可观测性：完整捕获 Claude / agent 的全部输出 log（可记录、可回放、持久化）— 满足 VM7（全量可观测/记录/回放/持久化）与 VM8（优雅关停时不丢日志）

options.name: 文件系统 JSONL session transcript（~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session-id>.jsonl）—— ground truth 基底层
options.description: Claude Code/Agent SDK 默认把每个 session 以 append-only JSONL 落盘:每行一条 typed record(user prompt、assistant 含 text/thinking/tool_use、tool_result、每轮 token usage、model、cwd、git 快照),用 parentUuid 串链。这是最完整、最权威的「全部输出」记录;append-only = 硬杀进程不丢已写数据(天然满足 VM8)。`claude --resume <id>` 可回放/续跑,simonw/claude-code-transcripts 可渲染 HTML。容器化只需把容器内 HOME 下 .claude/projects bind-mount/named-volume 到宿主。
pros: 零代码零额外服务:默认行为,开箱即有
内容最全:prompt + thinking + 每个 tool input/output + 每轮 token + model + git state
append-only,硬 kill 不丢已写 —— 对 VM8 优雅关停最稳
原生可回放(--resume),第三方工具可渲染 HTML
容器场景只需一个 bind mount 即跨边界持久化
cons: 行级 JSONL,人读需工具渲染(非现成 UI)
路径派生自 cwd,容器内需固定 HOME/工作目录以可预测
未跨 session 检索(要 UI 得叠 Langfuse)
options.isolation: 与隔离正交:容器内写文件 + bind mount,agent 无需联网;最适合 --network none 强隔离 agent
options.macFit: 极好:一个 -v 挂载即可

options.name: Headless stream-json(claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages)
options.description: headless 运行的实时事件流,newline-delimited JSON 每行一事件:system/init(model/tools/MCP/plugins)、assistant(reasoning+tool calls)、stream_event(token delta,需 --include-partial-messages)、system/api_retry(attempt/max_retries/error 分类)、terminal result(结果+total_cost_usd+per-model cost+session_id)。--output-format json 则一次性返回 result/session_id/usage/cost。`| tee /logs/run.jsonl` 即落盘可回放;--verbose 是拿全中间事件的必需项。
pros: 实时可观测:token delta/tool call/retry 全可见(VM7 可观测)
自带 session_id+cost+usage,便于按 run 归档与对账
纯 stdout JSON,tee 到挂载文件即持久化,无依赖
驱动后台/编排 agent 的标准接口
cons: --verbose 为拿全事件之必需
stdin 管道上限 10MB(大输入走文件)
仅 headless(-p)路径;交互式不走
options.isolation: 容器内运行,stdout 经 docker 捕获或 tee 到 bind mount
options.macFit: 极好:CLI + 管道

options.name: OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES=file:<dir> —— 原始 Messages API 请求/响应全量落盘
options.description: 开 CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1 后设 OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES=file:/logs/api,为每次 API attempt 写出未截断的 <uuid>.request.json(完整 system prompt + 全部 messages + tools)与 <request_id>.response.json(content blocks + usage + stop_reason),log event 里给 body_ref 指针。=1 则内联但截 60KB。这是「每一条发给 Claude / 每一条 Claude 返回」最硬核的全量证据(历史轮 thinking 被 redact)。
pros: 字面意义的全部 Claude 输出:untruncated API 请求体+响应体(file 模式)
每次重试单独成文件,审计/回放无死角
只是两个 env var,无需第三方平台
cons: 含完整对话历史 = 敏感,落盘目录需受控
历史轮 thinking 被 redact(非 thinking 全文)
文件量大,需轮转/清理
options.isolation: 写到容器内挂载目录,纯本地不依赖外发
options.macFit: 极好:env var + bind mount

options.name: Claude Code/Agent SDK 原生 OpenTelemetry(metrics+logs/events+traces beta)→ OTLP
options.description: CLI 内置 OTel:CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1 后,metrics(token/cost/session/active_time)、log events(user_prompt/tool_result/api_request/api_error/api_refusal/tool_decision/permission_mode_changed/auth/mcp_server_connection)、traces(beta,需 CLAUDE_CODE_ENHANCED_TELEMETRY_BETA=1:claude_code.interaction > llm_request/tool/hook span,subagent 嵌套成一条 trace)。经 OTEL_*_EXPORTER=otlp + OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT 发到任意 OTLP 后端。内容默认结构化,用 OTEL_LOG_USER_PROMPTS=1/OTEL_LOG_TOOL_DETAILS=1/OTEL_LOG_TOOL_CONTENT=1 打开正文。Agent SDK 经子进程 env 透传同一套配置,并自动注入 W3C TRACEPARENT 把 agent run 挂到调用方 trace 下。
pros: 官方原生、跨 SDK 升级稳定;一套 env 同时给指标/事件/链路
trace 把 prompt→LLM→tool→subagent 串成可回放时间线
subagent 委派链自动 nest(对 CEO→部门→执行者多 agent 极有用)
可发任意 OTLP 后端(Langfuse/Jaeger/Grafana/collector)
cons: traces 仍 beta,span 名/属性可能变
短命进程靠批量 flush,被硬杀可能丢 buffer —— 需调小 export interval 或优雅关停(与 VM8 相关)
SDK 模式下禁用 console exporter(会污染 stdout 消息通道)
正文需显式 opt-in
options.isolation: 需容器能外发到 collector/后端;强隔离下给一条到本机 collector 的白名单出口
options.macFit: 好:env 配置 + 本机 Jaeger/Collector/Langfuse 容器接收

options.name: Docker 日志驱动(local vs json-file)+ 轮转 —— stdout/stderr 持久化兜底
options.description: Docker 默认 json-file 把容器 stdout/stderr 按行标注来源+时间戳存 JSON;local 驱动更快、默认压缩+默认轮转(20M×5≈100MB)。生产必须显式配轮转。json-file 例:--log-driver json-file --log-opt max-size=20m --log-opt max-file=10 --log-opt compress=true。是「无论 agent 打印什么到 stdout 都持久化」的安全网。
pros: 对一切 stdout/stderr 通吃,零应用改动
自带轮转/压缩,磁盘可控
json-file 保留结构化+时间戳+来源标注,易后续转发
cons: 只是 stdout 字节流,非语义化 agent 视图
选 local 则不能用某些依赖 json-file 的转发器
不配 max-size 会无限增长撑爆磁盘
options.isolation: 守护进程级捕获,与隔离完全兼容,agent 无感知
options.macFit: 好:Docker Desktop 直接支持,可在 daemon.json 设全局默认

options.name: Langfuse(自托管,MIT 核心)—— 检索/回放/评估 UI(最佳「好用」层)
options.description: 开源 LLM/agent 可观测平台,Mac 上 git clone langfuse && docker compose up -d → http://localhost:3000(约 2-3 分钟 ready;bundle Postgres/ClickHouse/Redis/MinIO)。两条接入 Claude:(A)Agent SDK Python:pip 装 langfuse + openinference-instrumentation-claude-agent-sdk,ClaudeAgentSDKInstrumentor().instrument() 一行自动把 tool call+model completion 变 span 推到 Langfuse,得 agent-graph 视图;(B)native OTel:Claude Code OTLP 发到 Langfuse OTLP ingest 端点。对 Anthropic/Claude Agent SDK 一等支持,trace+成本+eval 同平台。另有 hook-based 「Trace Claude Code」方案(Stop hook 读 transcript 用 Python SDK 上报),适合纯 CLI 不改代码。
pros: 现成可检索/可回放 UI:逐 step 看 prompt/response/tool-call,跨 session 聚合
对 Claude Agent SDK 一等集成,multi-agent 扇出能看清委派链
MIT 自托管、compose 一把起,数据全留本机(契合无生产环境)
trace+成本+eval 同一平台
cons: v3 组件较多(ClickHouse/Redis/MinIO),比纯文件方案重
native OTel traces 是 beta;最顺是 Agent SDK Python instrumentor
持久化需给 compose 卷做备份
options.isolation: 本机另一组容器,agent 容器只需一条到 langfuse-web:3000 出口,符合白名单出网
options.macFit: 好:compose 直接跑,官方明确支持 Mac

options.name: OpenTelemetry Collector(本机 OTLP 汇聚枢纽,可选)
options.description: 本机起 all-in-one Jaeger 或 otel-collector 容器接 OTLP(4317 gRPC/4318 http),把 Claude Code metrics/logs/traces 落地+扇出(同时写文件 exporter + 转 Langfuse + 转 Prometheus)。SDK 模式下用它替代被禁用的 console exporter 本地查看。是「多路 telemetry 统一落盘+分发」中枢,MVP 非必需。
pros: 一处接收多处扇出(文件+Langfuse+Prometheus)
SDK 模式下本地查看 telemetry 的推荐方式
解耦 agent 与后端,后端可随时换
cons: 多一个组件与 pipeline yaml
MVP 阶段对单 agent 过度工程
options.isolation: 本机容器,agent 只需一条到 collector 出口白名单
options.macFit: 好:官方镜像 docker run 即可

options.name: Helicone / Phoenix(Arize)/ Braintrust / Laminar —— 备选第三方观测
options.description: Helicone:wire 级代理(改 base_url 5 分钟接入),但 2026-03 被 Mintlify 收购进入维护模式(可用无新特性);代理模式下 agent trace 退化成一串 LLM 调用。Phoenix(Arize,开源,OTel/OpenInference 原生,docker 自托管)、Braintrust(eval 强,偏云)、Laminar(Rust,OTel 原生)均可经 OTLP/OpenInference 接 Claude,但对 Claude Agent SDK 的成熟度与自托管体验不如 Langfuse,优先级更低。
pros: Helicone 接入最快(改 base_url),代理天然抓每次请求/响应
Phoenix/Laminar OTel 原生,可走与 native OTel 同管道
Braintrust eval 能力强
cons: Helicone 已维护模式 + 代理丢 agent-graph
代理路径需改 ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL(对凭证/出网有侵入)
对 Claude Agent SDK 现成集成不如 Langfuse
options.isolation: 代理模式需改 base_url 走代理出口
options.macFit: 中:多为云优先或自托管更重

recommendation.pick: 分层「防御纵深」组合:L0 文件系统 JSONL transcript(bind mount,ground truth)+ L1 raw API bodies(OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES=file)+ L2 Docker local/json-file 轮转(stdout 兜底)作为「保证全量可记录+可回放」的零依赖底座;再叠一个自托管 Langfuse(docker compose)作为「好用」的检索/回放/成本 UI。
recommendation.why: MVP 的「保证每个 Claude 输出都被记录+可回放」其实 L0+L1+L2 就完全达成,且零第三方服务、只需 bind mount + 几个 env var + docker log-opt —— 完美契合「最易在 Mac 上起」。其中 JSONL transcript 是 append-only,硬杀/断电不丢已写数据,是 VM8 优雅关停下最可靠的记录形态;raw API bodies 是「每一条发出/返回」的字面全量证据。Langfuse 是 MIT 自托管、对 Claude Agent SDK 一等集成、数据全留本机,是当下「好用」维度最优选;Helicone 已进维护模式,不作首选。关键认知:OTel 靠批量 flush,单靠它在硬 kill 下会丢 buffer,所以必须以文件 JSONL 作 ground truth,OTel/Langfuse 只作「好用层」而非唯一真相源。
recommendation.setupSketch:
1) 持久化 ground truth(零代码):运行 agent 容器时挂载两个卷 + 设两个 env:
   docker run \
     -v cc_sessions:/root/.claude \           # JSONL session transcript(全部 prompt/thinking/tool/usage)
     -v $PWD/logs:/logs \                      # raw API bodies + stream-json
     -e CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1 \
     -e OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES=file:/logs/api \  # 每次请求/响应完整 JSON 落盘
     --log-driver json-file --log-opt max-size=20m --log-opt max-file=10 --log-opt compress=true \
     <agent-image>
   (容器内固定 HOME,保证 ~/.claude/projects/<proj>/<session-id>.jsonl 可预测)

2) headless 运行同时落盘可回放事件流:
   claude -p "<task>" --output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages | tee /logs/run-$(date +%s).jsonl

3) 起自托管 Langfuse(好用 UI):
   git clone https://github.com/langfuse/langfuse && cd langfuse && docker compose up -d   # → http://localhost:3000

4) 接入 Langfuse(选一):
   (A) Agent SDK Python(agent-graph 最佳):
       pip install langfuse claude-agent-sdk openinference-instrumentation-claude-agent-sdk
       from openinference.instrumentation.claude_agent_sdk import ClaudeAgentSDKInstrumentor
       ClaudeAgentSDKInstrumentor().instrument()   # 配 LANGFUSE_HOST=http://localhost:3000 + pk/sk
   (B) native OTel(不改代码):
       export CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1 CLAUDE_CODE_ENHANCED_TELEMETRY_BETA=1
       export OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER=otlp OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=otlp OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=otlp
       export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=http/protobuf OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4318
       export OTEL_LOG_USER_PROMPTS=1 OTEL_LOG_TOOL_DETAILS=1 OTEL_LOG_TOOL_CONTENT=1   # 记正文

5) VM8 优雅关停不丢 telemetry:docker stop 发 SIGTERM + 宽限期;同时调小 export interval(OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL=1000 OTEL_LOGS_EXPORT_INTERVAL=1000 OTEL_TRACES_EXPORT_INTERVAL=1000)减少 buffer 丢失窗口;ground truth 靠 append-only JSONL 不受 flush 影响。

keyInsights: 「全量+可回放」的真正 ground truth 是文件系统 JSONL transcript(~/.claude/projects/<proj>/<session-id>.jsonl),不是任何第三方平台:它默认开启、append-only(硬杀不丢已写)、含 prompt/thinking/每个 tool input+output/每轮 token/model/git state,且 `claude --resume` 原生可回放。容器化只需 bind-mount 该目录。

「每一条发给 Claude 、每一条 Claude 返回」的字面全量证据 = OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES=file:<dir>(配 CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1):为每次 API attempt 写 untruncated <uuid>.request.json + <request_id>.response.json。只要两个 env var。注意历史轮 extended-thinking 会被 redact。

Claude Code/Agent SDK 原生内置 OpenTelemetry:metrics(token/cost/session)+ log events(user_prompt/tool_result/api_request/api_error/tool_decision 等)+ traces(beta,claude_code.interaction>llm_request/tool/hook,subagent 自动嵌套)。一套 OTEL_* env 同时控制三路信号;正文需 OTEL_LOG_USER_PROMPTS/OTEL_LOG_TOOL_DETAILS/OTEL_LOG_TOOL_CONTENT 显式 opt-in。
OTel 靠批量 flush(metrics 60s/logs+traces 5s 默认),进程被硬杀会丢 buffer —— 这直接影响 VM8。对策:调小 export interval 到 1000ms + docker stop 留足够 SIGTERM 宽限期;但最稳的保障仍是以 append-only JSONL 作真相源,OTel/UI 只作锡上添花。
stdout 兜底用 Docker 日志驱动:json-file 保留 JSON+时间戳且 `docker logs` 可读,local 更快+默认压缩+默认轮转。两者都必须显式 max-size/max-file,否则无限增长撑爆磁盘。

「好用 UI」层首选自托管 Langfuse(MIT,docker compose up → localhost:3000):对 Claude Agent SDK 一等集成(openinference-instrumentation-claude-agent-sdk 一行 instrument),能看清 multi-agent 委派链(扇出 ~7x token),数据全留本机。Helicone 已于 2026-03 被 Mintlify 收购进入维护模式,不作首选;Phoenix/Braintrust/Laminar 可走 OTLP 但优先级低于 Langfuse。
Agent SDK 会自动把 W3C TRACEPARENT 注入子进程,把整个 agent run(含子 agent 、子 agent 运行的 Bash)挂到调用方 trace 下 —— 对 v6 的 CEO→部门→执行者嵌套编排可得到单一贯穿 trace,与 infra-research 里的编排选型直接套合。
安全提醒:开 OTEL_LOG_USER_PROMPTS/RAW_API_BODIES 后落盘的是含完整对话+凭证上下文的敏感数据,需与 infra-research 的「凭证永不进 LLM context / 白名单出网」原则一致:日志卷仅本机、访问受控,不随 agent 出网。

dimension: Graceful shutdown 与容器生命周期（长跑 Claude agent，macOS + Docker Desktop）

options.name: Custom supervisor entrypoint (Agent SDK, traps SIGTERM → interrupt → drain → disconnect)
options.description: Do NOT run `claude -p` as PID1. Run a thin Python/TS host script that uses ClaudeSDKClient in streaming mode. It installs a SIGTERM/SIGINT handler; on signal it calls client.interrupt() (V1/Python only, returns immediately), drains receive_response() until the ResultMessage (subtype=error_during_execution) with a bounded timeout, then exits the `async with` so disconnect() flushes the CLI's session JSONL, then flushes app logs/state and exits 0. This is the only way to get a clean flush + child-process termination programmatically, because SIGTERM delivered straight to `claude` does NOT flush and orphans Bash-tool children.
pros: interrupt() cleanly halts the in-flight turn while the transcript stays valid (Python SDK first-class method)
You control the drain window for long Computer-Use / browser tool calls before exit
disconnect()/__aexit__ now waits up to 5s for the CLI subprocess to flush its JSONL (fixed in PR #642) — pin a recent SDK
Same script handles boot-time resume(session_id), so shutdown + recovery share one code path
cons: interrupt() is ClaudeSDKClient-only (not the one-shot query()); V2 unstable_v2_createSession still lacks interrupt() (issue #120, open)
interrupt() returns immediately and does NOT clear the buffer — you MUST drain receive_response() or the next turn reads stale messages
Requires writing/maintaining ~40 lines of host code vs a bare `claude -p`
options.isolation: Orthogonal to isolation; runs inside whatever container/sandbox you chose. Works identically in a plain Docker container.
options.macFit: Runs unchanged on Docker Desktop (engine is a Linux VM); no macOS-specific code.
options.setupEffort: Medium — small wrapper script, but it is the load-bearing piece.

options.name: tini as PID1 with process-group signalling (-g) — bundled, not just --init
options.description: Make tini PID1 to reap zombies and forward SIGTERM. Docker's built-in `--init` (or compose `init: true`) gives you tini but WITHOUT the -g flag. Bundle tini yourself with ENTRYPOINT ["tini","-g","--"] so SIGTERM is delivered to the whole process group — this is what kills orphaned Bash-tool shells and browser/Computer-Use child processes that otherwise get reparented to PID1 and linger.
pros: Reaps zombie processes (long-running agent spawns many Bash/tool children)
-g forwards the stop signal to the entire process group, not just the immediate child — solves the 'orphaned Bash children' problem from issue #29096
~10KB, zero config, available natively (Docker 1.13+)
cons: Docker's `--init`/`init: true` does NOT pass -g, so for process-group kill you must install tini in the image yourself
tini forwards the signal but does not itself flush agent state — still need the supervisor to do the SDK flush
If you use both your tini ENTRYPOINT and compose init:true you double-init; pick one (set init:false)
options.isolation: Reaping/PID-hygiene only; does not change isolation boundary.
options.macFit: Identical behavior on Docker Desktop's Linux VM.
options.setupEffort: Low — one ENTRYPOINT line (or one --init flag for the no--g baseline).

options.name: docker stop grace period tuning (stop_grace_period / -t, STOPSIGNAL)
options.description: `docker stop` sends SIGTERM (default STOPSIGNAL) then SIGKILL after a grace period — default 10s on Linux containers. 10s is too short to interrupt an in-flight LLM turn + drain a running tool call + flush JSONL. Raise it: `docker stop -t 60 <ctr>` or compose `stop_grace_period: 60s`. Keep STOPSIGNAL=SIGTERM. The supervisor must complete interrupt+drain+flush inside this window or it gets SIGKILLed (no flush).
pros: One setting buys the supervisor enough time to drain + flush
STOPSIGNAL/-t are first-class Docker primitives, no app changes
stop_grace_period in compose makes the window declarative and repeatable
cons: `docker kill` and `docker compose down` defaults can bypass/under-honor the window; use `docker stop`/`compose stop`
-t -1 (wait forever) risks a hung container if the drain never returns — always pair the SDK drain with its own asyncio timeout
Too-long a window slows orchestration scale-down
options.isolation: N/A.
options.macFit: Same defaults on Docker Desktop. macOS gotcha: quitting Docker Desktop (Cmd-Q) may not give containers full grace — run `docker compose stop` first.

options.name: Persistence + resume via mounted session JSONL transcript
options.description: The CLI appends the conversation transcript to ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-hash>/<session_id>.jsonl (append-only, per-event). Mount that path as a Docker volume so it survives container death — this IS the crash-recovery primitive. Checkpoint the current session_id to a small mounted state file every turn. On (re)boot, start with ClaudeAgentOptions(resume=session_id): the transcript is deserialized, files-read/commands/edits are reloaded into context and the model resumes. Optionally fork_session=True to keep the original as an immutable audit copy.
pros: Survives even SIGKILL/crash: the last fully-written turn is on the volume because writes are append-only
resume=session_id restores full context (history, files, tool results) — model picks up where it stopped
fork_session gives a clean branch while preserving the original transcript for replay/audit
Doubles as the persisted, replayable log store (overlaps the observability requirement)
cons: A turn that was mid-write at SIGKILL is lost (only the last COMPLETE turn is guaranteed)
Race in older SDKs: disconnect() could SIGTERM the CLI before the last assistant line flushed (issue #625) — fixed in PR #642, so pin a current SDK
session_id must be persisted yourself each turn for unattended resume after a crash
options.isolation: Volume crosses the isolation boundary by design; keep it agent-scoped (one volume per founder-agent).
options.macFit: Bind mount works on Docker Desktop (VirtioFS default); append-only JSONL is fine over VirtioFS. Named volume avoids FUSE entirely if you don't need host-side inspection.
options.setupEffort: Low — two volume mounts + a tiny session_id checkpoint.

options.name: Stop / SessionEnd hooks for extra-state checkpointing
options.description: Register a Stop hook (fires on agent execution stop; available in both SDKs) and/or a SessionEnd shell hook (TS SDK callback or, in Python, a shell-command hook loaded via setting_sources=['project'] from .claude/settings.json) to checkpoint app-level state — business memory/wiki pointer, current task, in-progress artifacts — to the mounted volume right before exit.
pros: Stop hook is the documented place to 'save session state before exit'
Hooks can fire async side-effects (logging/webhook) without blocking the agent
SessionEnd usable for temp-resource cleanup
cons: SessionEnd is NOT a Python SDK callback — Python must use a shell-command hook in settings.json (setting_sources=['project'])
Hooks may NOT fire on max_turns end, and SessionEnd does not fire on raw signal kill — do not rely on it as the only flush; the supervisor must also flush
Hook timeout default 60s; a slow hook can eat your grace window
options.isolation: N/A.
options.macFit: No macOS specifics.

options.name: HEALTHCHECK + restart policy (liveness + auto-recover)
options.description: Have the supervisor write a heartbeat timestamp to /state/heartbeat every N seconds. Add a Dockerfile/compose HEALTHCHECK that fails if the heartbeat is stale (e.g. >90s). Set restart: unless-stopped (or on-failure) so Docker Desktop relaunches a crashed/hung agent; on boot the supervisor resumes the persisted session_id. This closes the loop: liveness detection → restart → resume.
pros: Detects a wedged 'stuck Working' agent (a real failure mode) rather than only hard crashes
restart: unless-stopped + resume = self-healing long-runner on a dev Mac
Heartbeat file is trivial and survives in the mounted volume
cons: A liveness restart is a SIGKILL-class event if the container is wedged — may lose the in-flight turn (acceptable; resume covers it)
Healthcheck tuning needed so a legitimately long tool call isn't flagged unhealthy
restart loops can mask a persistent crash — add backoff/alerting
options.isolation: N/A.
options.macFit: HEALTHCHECK + restart policies run normally under Docker Desktop.

options.name: Anti-pattern: run `claude -p` headless directly as PID1
options.description: Running the headless CLI as the container's main process and relying on docker stop's SIGTERM to shut it down. Per feature request #29096, SIGTERM kills `claude` but does NOT fire SessionEnd hooks and ORPHANS all Bash-tool child processes (reparented to PID1); SIGKILL fires no hooks at all. There is no programmatic graceful-exit equivalent of /exit today.
pros: Simplest possible setup (no wrapper)
cons: SIGTERM does not flush session state cleanly; SessionEnd hooks only fire on interactive exit
Orphans Bash/tool/browser child processes
No bounded drain of in-flight tool calls
Feature request for a real graceful-shutdown signal is still open (closed-as-duplicate, unresolved)
options.isolation: N/A.
options.macFit: Same broken behavior on Docker Desktop.
options.setupEffort: Low — but do not use for a long-runner.

recommendation.pick: tini -g (PID1, reap + process-group SIGTERM) → a custom SDK supervisor (trap SIGTERM → interrupt → bounded drain → disconnect-flush → exit 0) → mounted JSONL transcript + resume(session_id) for crash recovery → stop_grace_period: 60s + HEALTHCHECK heartbeat + restart: unless-stopped
recommendation.why: On Docker Desktop the engine is a Linux VM, so standard signal semantics apply verbatim. The two failure modes that actually bite a long-running Claude agent are (1) SIGTERM sent straight to `claude` does NOT flush state and orphans Bash-tool children (issue #29096), and (2) the CLI subprocess can be killed before its last transcript line is flushed (issue #625, fixed in PR #642). The supervisor pattern fixes (1) by using the SDK's own interrupt()/disconnect() flush path instead of the raw signal, tini -g fixes orphaned children, a recent pinned SDK fixes (2), and the mounted append-only JSONL + resume() makes even an ungraceful SIGKILL recoverable. This is also the easiest to operate on a Mac: it is plain Docker — one Dockerfile, one compose file, `docker compose stop` for graceful, `restart: unless-stopped` for self-heal.
recommendation.setupSketch:
DOCKERFILE:
FROM python:3.12-slim
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends tini nodejs npm \
 && npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code \
 && pip install --no-cache-dir 'claude-agent-sdk>=<pin a version that includes PR #642>' \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
COPY supervisor.py /app/supervisor.py
STOPSIGNAL SIGTERM
# tini -g forwards SIGTERM to the whole process group (kills orphaned Bash/browser children)
ENTRYPOINT ["tini","-g","--"]
CMD ["python","-u","/app/supervisor.py"]
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD [ $(( $(date +%s) - $(cat /state/heartbeat 2>/dev/null || echo 0) )) -lt 90 ]

COMPOSE (docker-compose.yml):
services:
  founder-agent:
    build: .
    init: false                 # we ship tini -g ourselves; don't double-init
    stop_signal: SIGTERM
    stop_grace_period: 60s      # room for interrupt + drain + JSONL flush before SIGKILL
    restart: unless-stopped     # auto-relaunch on crash; supervisor resumes session_id
    environment: [ ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ]
    volumes:
      - claude-sessions:/root/.claude/projects   # transcript JSONL survives container death
      - ./state:/state                           # session_id ptr + heartbeat + structured logs
volumes: { claude-sessions: {} }

SUPERVISOR (supervisor.py, sketch):
import asyncio, signal, json, time, os
from claude_agent_sdk import ClaudeSDKClient, ClaudeAgentOptions, ResultMessage
STATE='/state/session.json'
def load_sid():
    try: return json.load(open(STATE)).get('session_id')
    except FileNotFoundError: return None
def save_sid(sid):
    if sid: json.dump({'session_id': sid}, open(STATE,'w'))
def heartbeat():
    open('/state/heartbeat','w').write(str(int(time.time())))
async def consume(client):
    async for m in client.receive_response():
        save_sid(getattr(m,'session_id',None)); heartbeat()
        # append m to /state/agent.log.jsonl with flush+fsync (replayable, SIGKILL-safe)
        if isinstance(m, ResultMessage): break
async def main():
    stop=asyncio.Event(); loop=asyncio.get_running_loop()
    for s in (signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIGINT): loop.add_signal_handler(s, stop.set)
    opts=ClaudeAgentOptions(resume=load_sid(), permission_mode='acceptEdits',
                            setting_sources=['project'])  # loads Stop/SessionEnd shell hooks
    async with ClaudeSDKClient(options=opts) as client:
        await client.query(NEXT_TASK_PROMPT)
        drain=asyncio.create_task(consume(client))
        await stop.wait()                 # blocks until docker stop -> SIGTERM
        await client.interrupt()          # halt in-flight turn (returns immediately)
        try: await asyncio.wait_for(drain, timeout=30)   # bounded drain of in-flight tool call
        except asyncio.TimeoutError: pass
    # __aexit__ -> disconnect() waits up to ~5s for CLI to flush its JSONL (PR #642)
asyncio.run(main())

OPERATE ON MAC:
  graceful stop:  docker compose stop founder-agent   # honors stop_grace_period
  AVOID:          docker kill / Cmd-Q Docker Desktop   # SIGKILL, no flush (resume still recovers)
  recover:        container restarts -> supervisor resume()s last session_id automatically

keyInsights: Docker Desktop on Mac runs the engine inside a Linux VM, so docker stop / SIGTERM / tini / --init / STOPSIGNAL behave exactly as on Linux — no macOS-specific signal handling is needed; the only Mac gotchas are at the CLI level (Cmd-Q quitting Docker Desktop and `docker kill` skip the grace period).

docker stop = SIGTERM (default STOPSIGNAL) then SIGKILL after a 10s grace period by default. 10s is too short for an in-flight LLM turn + tool drain + flush; set stop_grace_period: 60s (or docker stop -t 60). VERIFIED via docker docs.

PID1 ignores signals unless it registers a handler, and the kernel won't auto-reap its children. Use tini (built-in via --init, or bundled). Built-in --init does NOT pass tini's -g; bundle tini with ENTRYPOINT ["tini","-g","--"] to signal the whole process group and kill orphaned Bash/browser children. VERIFIED via krallin/tini.

Do NOT run `claude -p` headless as PID1: SIGTERM to it does NOT flush session state, does NOT fire SessionEnd hooks (those fire only on interactive exit), and ORPHANS all Bash-tool children. A programmatic graceful-exit signal is still an open, unresolved feature request (claude-code #29096).

The clean shutdown path is programmatic, not signal-driven: ClaudeSDKClient.interrupt() halts the current turn (Python/V1 first-class; V2 unstable_v2_createSession still lacks it per SDK-ts #120). interrupt() returns immediately and does NOT clear the buffer — you MUST drain receive_response() until the ResultMessage (subtype=error_during_execution) before exiting or sending a new query.

There was a real flush race: disconnect()/close() could SIGTERM the CLI subprocess before it wrote the last assistant line to the session JSONL (claude-agent-sdk-python #625). Fixed in PR #642 (disconnect now waits up to ~5s for graceful exit). Pin a current SDK version.

Crash recovery primitive = the append-only transcript JSONL at ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-hash>/<session_id>.jsonl. Mount it on a volume; checkpoint session_id every turn; on boot use ClaudeAgentOptions(resume=session_id) to restore full context. Even a SIGKILL only loses the turn that was mid-write — the last complete turn survives. fork_session=True keeps an immutable audit copy.

Liveness matters as much as shutdown: a Claude agent can wedge in 'Working' and ignore stop. Have the supervisor heartbeat to /state, add a HEALTHCHECK that fails on stale heartbeat, and set restart: unless-stopped so Docker Desktop relaunches and the supervisor auto-resumes — self-healing long-runner. Stop/SessionEnd hooks are a secondary checkpoint, not the primary flush (SessionEnd is not a Python SDK callback and never fires on raw signal kill).
